headline: GOLD STEADIES AT $4,700 As Iran Ceasefire Talks Advance; Silver Holds $76 on Deficit
snippet: Gold held near $4,700/oz on April 27 as US-Iran negotiations over the Strait of Hormuz blockage continued to underpin safe-haven demand; MCX futures firmed while silver steadied around $76/oz, supported by a sixth consecutive year of structural supply deficit and persistent industrial demand.

headline: UR-ENERGY STARTS URANIUM MINING At Shirley Basin — First New US ISR Operation In Over A Decade
snippet: Ur-Energy announced on April 23 that uranium-bearing solution is now being captured at Mine Unit 1 of its Shirley Basin ISR project in Wyoming, marking the first new in-situ recovery operation to start in the US in over a decade; combined licensed production and toll-processing capacity with Lost Creek now reaches 4.2 million pounds U₃O₈ annually.

headline: DISCOVERY SILVER DRILLS 770 g/t GOLD At Porcupine TVZ; 143 Holes Confirm District Scale
snippet: Discovery Silver released assays from 143 holes (44,740m) at Porcupine on April 23, with standout results including 72.46 g/t over 3.8m (incl. 770 g/t over 0.3m) at TVZ1 and 45.77 g/t over 4.1m at Hoyle Pond South; Q1 2026 production came in at 60,269 oz gold at 2.96 g/t with 90.6% recovery, keeping the company on track for 260,000–300,000 oz guidance.

headline: $63 MILLION EXPLORATION BLITZ: Contango Silver & Gold Plans 40,000m Drill at Kitsault Valley
snippet: Contango Silver & Gold outlined a $63 million 2026 exploration budget on April 24, with $25 million and 40,000 metres of infill drilling directed at its Kitsault Valley gold and critical metals project in Northern BC to upgrade resources; the remainder targets advanced projects across Alaska.

headline: MELKIOR STARTS 3,000m DRILL Targeting Bonanza Gold Zones at Carscallen, 25km From Timmins
snippet: Melkior Resources (TSXV: MKR) commenced a 10-hole, 3,000m program at its Carscallen Gold Property on April 24, focusing on the 1010 and Zamzam-Jowsey bonanza zones west of Timmins; one hole will test a new target 500m north of the 1010 North zone that has never been drilled, with first assays expected by end of June.

headline: CULLINAN METALS MOVES INTO LABRADOR RARE EARTHS With Option on Oculus Project
snippet: Cullinan Metals Corp. (CSE: CMT) entered an option agreement on April 24 to acquire the Oculus Rare Earth Project — 2,550 hectares in Central Labrador's recognized REE district, 110km northeast of Churchill Falls — plus the Burnt Pond VMS project in Newfoundland; the staged cash payments total $675,000.

headline: COT REPORT: Spec Gold Longs Hold Firm as Metal Consolidates Near $4,700 — GoldSeek April 24
snippet: GoldSeek's April 24 Commitments of Traders report finds speculative long positioning in gold remaining constructive despite the pullback from January's $5,589 all-time high, with commercial short pressure elevated but not historically extreme; silver speculative longs are also holding, consistent with further upside potential if industrial demand holds.

headline: SOUTHERN CROSS GOLD HITS 55.3 g/t + 1.9% ANTIMONY At Sunday Creek; Now Running 11 Rigs
snippet: Southern Cross Gold (TSX-V: SXG) reported on April 22 that hole SDDSC203 at its Sunday Creek project in Victoria intersected 1.6m at 55.3 g/t gold plus 1.9% antimony from 313.8m depth; a second hole hit samples exceeding 10% antimony with 227 g/t gold, and the company has now expanded to 11 drill rigs on site.

headline: COPPER SUPPLY SHOCK: Strait Closure Kills Acid Supply as China Bans Sulphuric Exports From May 1
snippet: Copper is up 24% year-over-year as the Strait of Hormuz blockage cuts off sulphuric acid — a critical input for roughly one-fifth of global refined copper output — while China simultaneously banning sulphuric acid exports from May 1 is tightening the market further; Goldman Sachs and Traxys are diverging on year-end price targets, with Traxys projecting $15,000/t.

headline: NdPr ALLOY UP 138% YTD, TERBIUM Surges 20.7% In April As Rare Earth Supply Deficit Deepens
snippet: NdPr alloy has surged 138% year-to-date in 2026 as the supply deficit enters its second consecutive year and EV demand is forecast at 22.9 million units globally; terbium posted its largest single-month gain since 2023 in April, rising 20.7% to $970/kg, underscoring how quickly the market is tightening beyond the headline NdPr move.
